perturbation.original_text: 8.1.1. Upon Discloserȇs request, Recipient shall promptly, and in any case within five (5) days from such request, return all documentation and other materials containing any Confidential Information of Discloser without retaining any copies thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1.1. Upon Discloser’s request, Recipient shall return all documentation and other materials containing any Confidential Information of Discloser without retaining any copies thereof within one (1) day from such request.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the return timeframe to one day may be unreasonably short, potentially conflicting with data protection laws depending on the volume and complexity of the data to be returned.
perturbation.contradicted_law: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) - Right to Erasure (Article 17)
perturbation.law_explanation: While GDPR focuses on erasure, the practicality of fulfilling data subject rights (including return of data if requested instead of erasure) is impacted by unreasonably short deadlines. A one-day deadline may not allow for proper verification and secure transfer, potentially leading to data breaches or non-compliance with GDPR's security requirements (Article 32).

perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a potential legal contradiction. The original contract allowed for five days to return confidential information, while the changed text reduces this timeframe to one day. The Law Explanation argues that such a short deadline may not allow for proper verification and secure transfer, potentially leading to data breaches or non-compliance with GDPR's security requirements. While no direct law snippet specifies a minimum timeframe for data return, the explanation suggests that a one-day deadline could be considered unreasonably short in the context of data protection laws, thus creating an inconsistency that violates data protection principles.

perturbation.original_text: 7.2.1. Each Party may terminate the Agreement by giving one month prior written notice to the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.2.1. Each Party may terminate the Agreement by giving one week prior written notice to the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the notice period to one week might conflict with standard commercial practice and legal expectations for reasonable notice, especially if the agreement involves ongoing obligations.
perturbation.law_explanation: Aftaleloven § 36 allows Danish courts to modify or invalidate contractual terms that are deemed unreasonable or unfair. A one-week termination notice could be considered unreasonable, particularly if the agreement involves significant investments or long-term collaborations. A court may adjust the notice period to a more reasonable duration.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Retsinformation You need to enable JavaScript to run this app.
perturbation.justification: The original contract provides for one month's notice for termination, while the changed text reduces this to one week. The Perturbation Explanation suggests this reduction might be unreasonable, and the Law Explanation cites Aftaleloven § 36, which allows courts to modify unfair contract terms. While the scraped snippets are limited, the Law Explanation strongly suggests that reducing the notice period could be deemed unreasonable under Danish law, creating a potential contradiction. The Inconsistencies Legal Contradiction definition is met because the one-week deadline violates legal expectations for reasonable notice.

perturbation.original_text: 7.3.1. The obligations of confidentiality, non-disclosure and non-use set forth in this Confidentiality Agreement shall survive the termination or expiration of this Confidentiality Agreement (i) with respect to any Confidential Information that constitutes a trade secret under applicable law, for so long as such item shall continue to constitute a trade secret under applicable law, and (ii) with respect to any Confidential Information that does not constitute a trade secret under applicable law, for a period of 5 (five) years from and after the date of disclosure of such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.3.1. The obligations of confidentiality, non-disclosure and non-use set forth in this Confidentiality Agreement shall survive the termination or expiration of this Confidentiality Agreement (i) with respect to any Confidential Information that constitutes a trade secret under applicable law, for so long as such item shall continue to constitute a trade secret under applicable law, and (ii) with respect to any Confidential Information that does not constitute a trade secret under applicable law, for a period of 6 months from and after the date of disclosure of such Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the confidentiality period for non-trade secret information to 6 months could be seen as insufficient, especially considering standard commercial practices and potentially invalidating the agreement.
perturbation.law_explanation: Markedsføringsloven § 3 prohibits unfair commercial practices, which include the disclosure of confidential information obtained through a business relationship. While not directly specifying a confidentiality duration, a 6-month period may be deemed insufficient to protect commercially sensitive information, potentially conflicting with the implied duty of good faith and fair dealing under Danish law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Lov om kontroll med markedsføring og avtalevilkår mv. (markedsføringsloven) - Lovdata Til hovedinnhold Verktøylinje Lov om kontroll med markedsføring og avtalevilkår mv. (markedsføringsloven)  ⎙  ⎙ Skriv ut  Del dokument Opprinnelig kunngjort versjon Opprinnelig kunngjort versjon XML-versjon ➦ Gå til ajourført versjon Lov om kontroll med markedsføring og avtalevilkår mv. (markedsføringsloven) Dato LOV-2009-01-09-2 Departement Barne- og likestillingsdepartementet Publisert I 2009 hefte 1 s 6 Ikrafttredelse Kongen bestemmer. Endrer LOV-1972-06-16-47 , LOV-1973-03-09-14 , LOV-1981-12-18-90 , LOV-1989-06-02-27 , LOV-1992-12-04-127 , LOV-2000-04-14-31 , LOV-2000-12-21-105 Kunngjort 09.01.2009 kl. 14.05 Rettet 13.01.2009 (EØS-henv.) Journalnr 2008-1003 Korttittel Markedsføringsloven Ot.prp.nr.55 (2007–2008) , Innst.O.nr.20 (2008–2009) og Besl.O.nr.14 (2008–2009) . Odels- og lagtingsvedtak hhv. 28. november og 4. desember 2008. Fremmet av Barne- og likestillingsdepartementet. Jf. EØS-avtalen vedlegg IX nr. 30d og vedlegg XIX nr. 7g, nr. 2, nr. 3a, nr. 7d og nr. 7f (direktiv 2005/29/EF ). Følgende lov oppheves: Lov 16. juni 1972 nr. 47 om kontroll med markedsføring og avtalevilkår (markedsføringsloven). Endringer i følgende lover: 1 Lov 9. mars 1973 nr. 14 om vern mot tobakksskader (tobakksskadeloven). 2 Lov 18. desember 1981 nr. 90 om merking av forbruksvarer mv. 3 Lov 2. juni 1989 nr. 27 om omsetning av alkoholholdig drikk mv. (alkoholloven). 4 Lov 4. desember 1992 nr. 127 om kringkasting. 5 Lov 14. april 2000 nr. 31 om behandling av personopplysninger (personopplysningsloven). 6 Lov 21. desember 2000 nr. 105 om opplysningsplikt og angrerett mv. ved fjernsalg og salg utenfor fast utsalgssted (angrerettloven). Kapittel 1 Alminnelige bestemmelser § 1. Hva loven gjelder Loven gjelder kontroll med markedsføring, handelspraksis og avtalevilkår i forbrukerforhold og stiller krav til god forretningsskikk mellom næringsdrivende. 🔗 Del paragraf § 2. God markedsføringsskikk mv. Markedsføring må ikke stride mot god markedsføringsskikk. Ved vurderingen legges det vekt på om markedsføringen krenker allmenne etikk- og moraloppfatninger, eller om det tas i bruk støtende virkemidler. Annonsør og den som utformer reklame, skal sørge for at reklamen ikke er i strid med likeverdet mellom kjønnene, og at den ikke utnytter det ene kjønns kropp eller gir inntrykk av en støtende eller nedsettende vurdering av kvinne eller mann. Ved vurderingen av om første eller annet ledd er overtrådt, legges det vekt på om markedsføringen, på grunn av utforming, format, omfang eller andre virkemidler, fremstår som særlig påtrengende. 🔗 Del paragraf § 3. Presentasjon og dokumentasjon av markedsføring Markedsføring skal utformes og presenteres slik at den tydelig framstår som markedsføring. Påstander i markedsføring om faktiske forhold, herunder om ytelsers egenskaper eller virkning, skal kunne dokumenteres. Dokumentasjonen skal foreligge på annonsørens hånd når markedsføringen skjer. 🔗 Del paragraf § 4. Lovens stedlige virkeområde Loven gjelder for handlinger og vilkår som er rettet mot forbrukere eller næringsdrivende her i riket, med de begrensninger som følger av annen lovgivning. Med unntak av kapittel 6 gjelder loven også for handlinger og vilkår som har virkning i utlandet, når de er ulovlige også etter lovgivningen i landet der de har virkning. Kongen kan
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the confidentiality period for non-trade secret information from 5 years to 6 months. The Law Explanation states that Markedsføringsloven § 3 prohibits unfair commercial practices, including disclosing confidential information obtained through a business relationship. While the scraped snippet of Markedsføringsloven doesn't specify a minimum confidentiality duration, it does generally address good business practice. Reducing the period to 6 months could be deemed insufficient to protect commercially sensitive information and thus be considered a violation of the general standard outlined in the law snippet regarding good business practice.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1.1. Upon Discloserȇs request, Recipient shall promptly, and in any case within five (5) days from such request, return all documentation and other materials containing any Confidential Information of Discloser without retaining any copies thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1.1. Upon Discloser’s request, Recipient may choose not to return all documentation and other materials containing any Confidential Information of Discloser without retaining any copies thereof.
perturbation.explanation: Allowing the recipient to choose not to return confidential information upon request contradicts the fundamental purpose of a Non-Disclosure Agreement, which is to protect sensitive data.
perturbation.law_explanation: This modification undermines the reasonable measures taken to protect trade secrets. By allowing the recipient to choose not to return confidential information, it increases the risk of misappropriation, violating the protections afforded by the Trade Secrets Act. Even if the information is not technically a trade secret, the failure to return it upon request can still be a breach of contract and fiduciary duty.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 18 U.S. Code § 1836 - Civil proceedings | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 18 U.S. Code § 1836 - Civil proceedings U.S. Code Notes prev | next (a) The Attorney General may, in a civil action, obtain appropriate injunctive relief against any violation of this chapter. (b) Private Civil Actions.— (1) In general.— An owner of a trade secret that is misappropriated may bring a civil action under this subsection if the trade secret is related to a product or service used in, or intended for use in, interstate or foreign commerce. (2) Civil seizure.— (A) In general.— (i) Application.— Based on an affidavit or verified complaint satisfying the requirements of this paragraph, the court may, upon ex parte application but only in extraordinary circumstances, issue an order providing for the seizure of property necessary to prevent the propagation or dissemination of the trade secret that is the subject of the action. (ii) Requirements for issuing order.— The court may not grant an application under clause (i) unless the court finds that it clearly appears from specific facts that— (I) an order issued pursuant to Rule 65 of the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure or another form of equitable relief would be inadequate to achieve the purpose of this paragraph because the party to which the order would be issued would evade, avoid, or otherwise not comply with such an order; (II) an immediate and irreparable injury will occur if such seizure is not ordered; (III) the harm to the applicant of denying the application outweighs the harm to the legitimate interests of the person against whom seizure would be ordered of granting the application and substantially outweighs the harm to any third parties who may be harmed by such seizure; (IV) the applicant is likely to succeed in showing that— (aa) the information is a trade secret ; and (bb) the person against whom seizure would be ordered— (AA

perturbation.justification: The modified text directly contradicts the purpose of a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA). The original text mandates the return of confidential information upon the Discloser's request, whereas the changed text allows the Recipient to 'choose not to return' the information. This directly undermines the protections afforded to trade secrets, as highlighted in the Law Explanation. Scraped Law Snippet 1, 18 U.S. Code § 1836, addresses civil proceedings for trade secret misappropriation, implying the existence of laws protecting trade secrets, the protection of which is undermined by the modified contract. Allowing the recipient to choose whether or not to return confidential data increases the risk of misappropriation and directly conflicts with the intent to protect trade secrets, which is a key component of many NDAs.
